url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation — Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §27 + §19 + §21.A house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-1 figures (FA 1998 Sch 18 para 17 £200/£400/£1000/£2000) carried verbatim from Stage 1 author verification on 2026-05-27.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified TMA 1970 s.7 / s.8 / Sch 55 + Sch 56 FA 2009 / FA 1998 Sch 18 para 17 (verbatim £200/£400/£1000/£2000) / SI 2021/1076 anchors at seed time on 2026-05-27. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §27 + §19 + §21.A locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review. The Stage 1 author's discipline reduces Stage 2 cross-check load to verification of brief-coherence rather than first-pass statute lookup.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers, leaving Stage 2 with substantive worked-example + FAQ + voice/workflow stub work but not statutory-rewriting. Each extended brief landed at +135 to +142 lines of additional content (worked examples + 12 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 2 author entry). This pattern suggests Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_ANTI_CANNIBAL_ANCHOR_LOAD — three picks in this batch (A2 DDS umbrella + A3 LPC benefits + A4 paper-vs-online) sit alongside 5-7 sibling MTD or LPC pages elsewhere in MW3 Bucket A. The framing-differentiator discipline carried in from Stage 1 makes the Stage 2 anti-templating note explicit per pick. RUN sessions for these three picks should re-read the sibling Stage 2 briefs before writing to confirm structural distinctness.

D-4 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause needed; no Q-N raised; no blocker encountered. ~1.5-2 hour wall-clock for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker. Confirms the leaf-agent pattern is operationally tight for Stage 2 batches of this size.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk for the pick (PAYE → general SA penalty; DDS → LPC-specific; LPC benefits → descriptive LPC; SA online-vs-paper → MTD-architecture; CT penalties → PAYE+SA penalty siblings; £20k MTD → other 7 MTD pages). No same-sentence drift detected across the set.
worktree: C:/Users/user/Documents/Accounting (main repo, Stage 2 commits to main per dispatch prompt)
notes: All 6 picks extended on main branch. Each commit message uses 'MW3 Stage 2 M3-A-B1 pick N/6: <slug> extended' pattern for grep-driven manager triage. No Q&A raised; no flags raised; no drift catches at Stage 2 cross-check (Stage 1 author's per-citation verification on 2026-05-27 holds clean). F-1 (FA 1998 Sch 18 para 17 figures site-wide back-patch) remains the operative flag from Stage 1 already raised in MW3 flags file; Stage 2 confirms brief-side alignment with verified figures.
